title: Beauchamp: Peter's Quartet & Gus King

main_route.excerpt: William, John, Charles, Simon + Eve left home. (Thomas at Greenbelt.) We didn't arrive at Beauchamp until 7.0. because of a jam on the M5.

geocode_hint.hint: Beauchamp House recurring music-course venue in Gloucestershire; M5 route from Birmingham consistent with Gloucestershire location; same venue as 1992, 1993, 1994, 1996 Beauchamp trips in this diary series

main_route.excerpt: We went home after a cup of tea.

excursions.label: Saturday visit to Gus King's house

stops.excerpt: We are visiting Gus King at 10.30. Wendy + the children are in Cornwall. Their house is lovely — half Tudor + half Victorian, moated, chickens. We picked blackberries.

geocode_hint.hint: Moated house, half Tudor half Victorian, accessible as a day trip from Beauchamp House (Gloucestershire); exact location unknown

unresolved.issue: Gus King is a named individual whose moated, half-Tudor/half-Victorian house was visited as a day trip from Beauchamp. His wife Wendy and children were in Cornwall at the time. The precise location of the house is not given; it is somewhere accessible as a day outing from Beauchamp (Gloucestershire). Cannot geocode with confidence.
unresolved.excerpt: We are visiting Gus King at 10.30. Wendy + the children are in Cornwall. Their house is lovely — half Tudor + half Victorian, moated, chickens.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0219_R opens with the tail end of the preceding trip ('check tyre pressure. We picked up Tonic + arrived home at 4.30.') before the 'August 22nd Friday' heading that begins this trip.

mixed_end: page A_0219_R also contains a 'Saturday September 6th' entry (1997-09-06) recording a day trip to Malmesbury while listening to Princess Diana's funeral on the radio. This is a separate outing and is not part of the Aug 22-23 Beauchamp trip. It has been excluded from this route.
Thomas is explicitly noted as being at Greenbelt (a Christian music festival) on August 22nd and is not a traveller on this trip.

Peter was already at Beauchamp (likely attending the music course before the family's Aug 22 arrival) — he is not listed among those who 'left home' at 5:25, but participates in the Gus King excursion on Aug 23 ('We are visiting Gus King') and performed in the concert ('Peter's concert went well').
summary: On Friday 22 August 1997, Simon, Eve, William, John and Charles drove from Birmingham to Beauchamp House (a recurring music-course venue in Gloucestershire), arriving late at 7pm due to an M5 jam; Peter was already there and played Haydn in a string quartet that evening. On Saturday 23rd the family made a day visit to the home of Gus King — a moated, half-Tudor, half-Victorian house with chickens — before returning to Birmingham after Peter's afternoon concert.
extraction_notes: Single diary page A_0219_R (diary p.28). The page is boundary-mixed at both start and end: the opening lines belong to the preceding trip (arrival home with 'Tonic' the dog), and the 'Saturday September 6th' section belongs to a separate subsequent outing (Malmesbury, Princess Diana's funeral radio broadcast) which is not included here. Beauchamp is identified as the recurring Beauchamp House music-course venue in Gloucestershire, consistent with the 1992, 1993, 1994, and 1996 Beauchamp trips in this diary series; the M5 jam routing from Birmingham supports the Gloucestershire location. Peter is not listed among those departing home at 5:25 — he was evidently already at Beauchamp attending the music course. The Gus King house visit is an excursion from Beauchamp; the house (moated, half Tudor/half Victorian) cannot be geocoded without further research. Thomas attended Greenbelt independently and is excluded from the travellers list. No crossings of any kind; entirely domestic England trip.
